findings.description: Launcher seeds swarm-state.json/child-agents.jsonl with truncating writes; commander-002 reports telemetry truncation and bundle/results missing
findings.evidence: Non-atomic writes in stampede.sh and run artifacts note truncated child-agents.jsonl; bundle/results required by contract absent

findings.description: Orphan recovery re-queues via write+delete (non-atomic) and monitor only reclaims when live_agents==0, risking duplicate execution or stuck claims
findings.evidence: SKILL.md orphan sweep uses write+remove; monitor gates recovery on live_agents==0

findings.description: Status enums and result schemas diverge (success/partial vs done/completed); synthesizer treats commander success as warning
findings.evidence: Commander contract uses success/partial; SKILL treats only done/completed as success

findings.description: Bundle schema omits source_refs and adopted_consensus_item_ids though launcher/protocol require them; runtime bundles use inconsistent field names
findings.evidence: Protocol requires source_refs; bundles vary between adopted_consensus_item_ids and adopted_consensus

findings.description: Monitor renders partial as failure and launcher uses blocked status outside commander contract; run-level status can conflict with commander success
findings.evidence: Monitor only ✅ for done/success; launcher sets blocked status; run state blocked while commander results success

findings.description: Monitor counts all results/*.json, including non-task artifacts, inflating done totals
findings.evidence: results/ contains sq-028-completion.json and atoms alongside task results

summary: Audit found multiple QA gaps: non-atomic seeding of commander artifacts can truncate telemetry and commander-002 lacks required bundle/results; claimed cleanup uses non-atomic requeue and monitor only recovers when all agents are dead; result schemas/status enums drift between worker/commander/skill; consensus/source_refs fields are required by launcher/protocol but missing or inconsistent in schema docs; partial/blocked statuses are inconsistently rendered; and monitor counts non-task results. Worker result writes are otherwise atomic.
